incentive.statement: Ordinary NNC noun class must use one profile-aware typed contract, keep Nawat and Classical spellings distinct, and never let raw formula strings or invalid explicit input manufacture class authority.

coordinator.responsibility: Consolidate noun-class recognition, orthographic projection, UI and URL parity, and hostile authorization proof without merging contextual class guidance, subclasses, number dyads, or pronominal NNC categories.

outcome: One core contract owns the ordered Nawat and Classical ordinary-NNC vocabularies, profile-specific aliases, and exact bridge; absent, recognized, and invalid intent remain distinct, and raw manually written formula strings are diagnostic-only.

responsibilities.statement: Own both profile vocabularies, aliases, typed selection frames, orthographic bridge, and engine fail-closed behavior.

actors.contribution: Implemented the profile-aware contract and removed invalid or string-derived class authorization.

responsibilities.statement: Derive state, active Result controls, composer handling, profile bridge, and URL restoration from typed class frames.

actors.contribution: Removed duplicate carriers, kept manual source text non-authorizing, and blocked malformed restored intent.

responsibilities.statement: Prove canonical and alias parity, Nawat and Classical separation, malformed direct and URL input blocking, manual-string non-authorization, fixture independence, and live UI behavior.

actors.contribution: Added hostile cross-layer tests, exact inventory checks, cache-busted runtime proof, and live browser verification.

responsibilities.statement: Record the bounded comparison, exclusions, proof, and remaining main finding.

actors.contribution: Maintained the shared-action boundary and final risk reminder.

contracts.invariant: Nawat and Classical class tokens normalize only in their declared profile; typed lexical fixtures may fill absence but never override invalid explicit intent; raw formula strings cannot supply class provenance.

retirements.removalEvidence: Direct engine tests reject unknown class intent for open stems and fixtures; state tests reject forged s-class and cn-class restoration; manual formula text no longer supplies class state; browser entry 052 showed no default class, exact profile controls, and retained typed state against a forged class URL.

closure.detail: Triplicated class recognition, invalid-to-default authorization, and raw-formula class provenance no longer decide ordinary NNC generation.

evidence: Focused proof passed: ordinary and Classical NNC suites 458/458, state 411/411, UI 411/411, derivation 224/224, Classical VNC application 32/32, orthography 41/41, grammar registry 43/43, and runtime 9/9.
Runtime manifest remained one browser entry with 75 modules and 75 installers; syntax, JSON, and diff checks passed.

Live entry 052 exposed Nawat t/ti/in/zero and Classical tl/tli/in/zero with all four Canvas option tags; a manual parenthesized stem selected no class by itself, and explicit typed class plus animacy remained the separate authorization path.
The forged /s-class/fabricated URL created no fabricated control and retained the prior typed t selection; the live browser loaded the 052 entry module.

Alignment architecture metrics matched baseline exactly. The repository-wide command remains red on inherited board size and cross-action dirty-worktree overlap, with no noun-class-attributable metric regression.
